title: Aylostera walteri
taxonomy: Aylostera walteri is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a part of the subgenus Milchkaktus within the genus Aylostera. The species is further classified as being within the section Aylostera. Aylostera walteri is native to Brazil and is characterized by its cylindrical stems and small white flowers. The classification of Aylostera walteri aligns with the broader classification of cacti under the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Aylostera walteri, also known as Walter's Living Stone, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae, characterized by its small size and unique, living rock-like appearance. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce oxidative stress, alleviate pain, and prevent the growth of certain microorganisms. The bioactive constituents of Aylostera walteri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Aylostera walteri is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations.
